t1: Truncation doesn't just preserve the fold — it improves it. Full STRC (1-1775) scores pTM 0.63 with 16 % disorder. Mini-STRC (616-1775, first-pass) jumps to pTM 0.81 at 7 % disorder. Ultra-Mini (1075-1775, the clinical candidate) reaches pTM 0.87 at 6 % disorder — the best fold across all 22 AF3 jobs. The disordered half was actively destabilising the protein; the LRR linker was structurally non-essential. Li et al. (2026) showed that AF3 builds structural constraints hierarchically: intra-chain geometry is established first, then inter-chain contacts are inferred from it. A large disordered N-terminus disrupts this process — removing it lets AF3 establish consistent geometric constraints across the entire chain, explaining the pTM progression 0.63 → 0.81 → 0.87.
t2: 6. AlphaFold 3 confirms: removing the N-terminal and the LRR linker both improve the fold (0.63 → 0.81 → 0.87)
